{"query":"Japan prime minister 2026 current name official Kantei profile","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1384,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sanae_Takaichi","title":"Sanae Takaichi - Wikipedia","content":"The native form of this personal name is Takaichi Sanae.
Sanae Takaichi (高市 早苗, Takaichi Sanae; born 7 March 1961) is a Japanese politician who has been Prime Minister of Japan and President of the Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) since October 2025.
In the 2003 Japanese general election, she was defeated in the Nara 1st district by Democratic Party lawmaker Sumio Mabuchi.
After becoming prime minister on 21 October, Takaichi formed her first cabinet.
2026 general election and second cabinet
She was especially popular among young voters, with one poll finding that 84 percent of respondents in their 20s and 78 percent of those in their 30s backed the prime minister and her cabinet (compared with 67 percent of voters overall).
Takaichi, like all other candidates in the 2025 LDP leadership election, supports revising article nine of the Japanese constitution to include mention of the Japan Self-Defence Forces.
Archived from the original on 18 August 2020.
"Fumio Kishida set to be new Japanese PM after winning party election".
"Who is Minoru Kihara, Takaichi's new 'cool-headed' spokesman?".
"Sanae Takaichi: the new leader of Japan's Liberal Democratic party who cites Thatcher as an influence"."},{"url":"https://japan.kantei.go.jp/105/actions/202602/_00001.html","title":"Designation of the Prime Minister (The Prime Minister in Action) | Prime Minister's Office of Japan","content":"On February 18, 2026, in the afternoon, a vote to designate the Prime Minister was held in both the House of Representatives and the House of Councillors.
TAKAICHI Sanae was designated as the Prime Minister, becoming the 105th Prime Minister of Japan, counting from ITO Hirobumi, the first Prime Minister."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2pmy7m72lo","title":"Sanae Takaichi set to become Japan's first female prime minister","content":"Japan's ruling conservative party has elected Sanae Takaichi as its new leader, positioning the 64-year-old to be Japan's first female prime minister.
Takaichi has been a long-time admirer of Britain's first female prime minister, Margaret Thatcher.
"She calls herself Japan's Margaret Thatcher.
Takaichi is a staunch conservative who's long opposed legislation allowing women to keep their maiden names after marriage, saying it is against tradition."},{"url":"https://japan.kantei.go.jp/","title":"Prime Minister's Office of Japan","content":"Prime Minister Takaichi Spoke to the Press Regarding the Fiscal 2026 Supplementary Budget Taking into Account the Situation in the Middle East"},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Japan","title":"Prime Minister of Japan - Wikipedia","content":"Sanae Takaichi succeeded Shigeru Ishiba as prime minister on 21 October 2025, following the 2025 Liberal Democratic Party presidential election; she is the first woman to serve as prime minister.
The office was replaced in 1885 with the appointment of Itō Hirobumi to the new position of Minister President of State, four years before the enactment of the Meiji Constitution, which mentions neither the Cabinet nor the position of Prime Minister explicitly.
Conventionally, since the prime minister must maintain the confidence of the Diet to stay in office, they are almost always the leader of the majority party in the House of Representatives or the leader of the senior partner in the governing coalition.
The original Kantei served from 1929 until 2002, when a new building was inaugurated to serve as the current Kantei.
The Lexus LS 600h L, which served as the prime minister's official car from 2008 to 2019, became a spare/alternative vehicle used by the Prime Minister."}]}

For a list of officeholders, see List of prime ministers of Japan. | Emblem of the prime minister of Japan |. | Standard of the prime minister of Japan |. | Executive branch of the Japanese government Office of the Prime Minister |. The **prime minister of Japan** (内閣総理大臣, *Naikaku Sōri Daijin*; Japanese pronunciation: ) is the head of government of Japan. The prime minister chairs the Cabinet of Japan and has the ability to select and dismiss its ministers of state. The office was replaced with the office of prime minister on 22 December 1885 with the appointment of Itō Hirobumi to the new position. The modern constitution, adopted in 1947, transferred formal executive powers from the emperor to the prime minister, as well as designating the Diet as the nominator of the prime minister instead of the emperor. See also: List of prime ministers of Japan and List of prime ministers of Japan by time in office.","score":0.37175277,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.13,"request_id":"069a4c8b-515a-4326-9daf-3b824361fbc9"}
